content.text:
export interface Experiment {
  href: string;
  title: string;
  summary: string;
  presets?: string[];
}

export const presetAtlas = ['quiet lattice', 'golden bloom', 'deep spiral', 'edge storm'];

export const experiments: Experiment[] = [
  { href: '/braid', title: 'braid group composer', summary: 'strand crossings, words, knot closures' },
  { href: '/cellular', title: 'cellular automata lab', summary: 'local rules, seeds, histories' },
  { href: '/complex', title: 'complex dynamics', summary: 'mandelbrot, julia, orbit traces' },
  { href: '/continued-fractions', title: 'continued fractions', summary: 'expansions, convergents, errors' },
  { href: '/chu-spaces', title: 'Chu spaces', summary: 'dual tables, tests, morphisms' },
  { href: '/dual', title: 'dual numbers', summary: 'nilpotents, tangents, automatic differentiation' },
  { href: '/fib/10', title: 'fibonacci', summary: 'static bigint sequence sample' },
  { href: '/fourier', title: 'Fourier epicycle sketcher', summary: 'draw, decompose, replay with epicycles' },
  { href: '/groups', title: 'finite groups', summary: 'tables, classes, quotients' },
  { href: '/hyperbolic', title: 'hyperbolic tessellation explorer', summary: 'poincaré disk, {p,q} tilings, geodesics' },
  { href: '/knot', title: 'knot diagrams', summary: 'crossings and polynomial sketches' },
  { href: '/lsystem', title: 'L-system garden', summary: 'rewrite rules, turtle paths, fractal plants' },
  { href: '/modular', title: 'modular multiplication circles', summary: 'residue chords, primitive roots, cycles' },
  { href: '/morse', title: 'Morse landscape explorer', summary: 'height fields, critical points, gradient flow' },
  { href: '/padic', title: 'p-adics', summary: 'digits, inverses, hensel lifts' },
  { href: '/penrose', title: 'Penrose tiling studio', summary: 'inflation, matching rules, aperiodic tiles' },
  { href: '/primes', title: 'prime spiral observatory', summary: 'ulam spiral, residue classes, progressions' },
  { href: '/reaction-diffusion', title: 'reaction-diffusion lab', summary: 'gray-scott patterns, feed/kill map' },
  { href: '/spectral', title: 'spectral graph playground', summary: 'laplacian spectra, eigenmodes, layouts' },
  { href: '/surreal', title: 'surreal numbers', summary: 'cuts and finite birthdays' },
  { href: '/symmetry', title: 'symmetry', summary: 'rotational mirror drawing' },
  { href: '/tropical', title: 'tropical geometry', summary: 'min-plus arithmetic and envelopes' },
  { href: '/voronoi', title: 'voronoi', summary: 'distance fields with mixed metrics' },
];

content.text:
The shared preset atlas is:

- `quiet lattice`
- `golden bloom`
- `deep spiral`
- `edge storm`

Defined in `src/data/experiments.ts`.

Other page-specific defaults include:

- Fourier: no default unless `?preset=...` is provided
- L-system: `koch`
- Reaction diffusion: `coral`
- Penrose: `star` / “star wheel”
remove preset from the experiments where they are not used

Done.

Changed:
- `src/layouts/CanvasLayout.astro`
  - Removed always-on shared preset dropdown.
  - Added optional `presets?: string[]` prop.
  - Renders preset control only when presets are provided.

- `src/pages/fourier.astro`
  - Passes `presetAtlas` to `CanvasLayout`, since Fourier actually uses those presets.

Verified with:
- `bun run build` ✅

<skill name="commit" location="/home/resu/.agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md">
References are relative to /home/resu/.agents/skills/commit.

# Commit Local Changes

You are helping create a git commit for the current repository.

## Guardrails

- Do not push.
- Do not discard, revert, reset, or overwrite user changes.
- Do not commit secrets, credentials, editor junk, build artifacts, dependency folders, or ignored files unless the user explicitly requests it.
- Preserve intentional staging: if files are already staged, commit the staged changes and only add more files when the user's request clearly includes them.
- If staging is ambiguous, ask the user instead of guessing.

## Workflow

1. Inspect repository state:
   - `git status --short --branch`
   - `git diff --stat`
   - `git diff`
   - `git diff --staged --stat`
   - `git diff --staged`
2. If there are no staged or unstaged changes, report that there is nothing to commit and stop.
3. Decide what to stage:
   - If changes are already staged, keep the staged set unless the user asked to include additional files.
   - If nothing is staged, stage the appropriate tracked and untracked files with `git add -- <paths>`.
   - Prefer path-specific `git add -- <paths>` over broad `git add .`.
4. Re-check exactly what will be committed:
   - `git status --short`
   - `git diff --staged --stat`
   - `git diff --staged`
5. Choose a concise commit message based on the staged diff:
   - Follow the repository's existing style if obvious from recent commits (`git log --oneline -5`).
   - Otherwise use a short imperative summary, optionally with a body for multi-area changes.
6. Run `git commit -m "<message>"` (use additional `-m` lines for a body when useful).
7. Report:
   - commit hash (`git rev-parse --short HEAD`)
   - commit message
   - any uncommitted changes left behind
</skill>
